title: ggplot2 demo
text:
Figure 1 further explores the impact of temperature on ozone level.


Show code
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(airquality, aes(Temp, Ozone)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method="loess")








Figure 1: Temperature and ozone level.

title: NYC Education Analytics
text:
This project explores NYC Open Data education datasets using R for statistical analysis and visualization. All analysis is written in Org-mode for literate programming and exported to Quarto for publishing.


We use the NYC Open Data Education Portal which provides:

School locations and building information
Student enrollment and demographics
Test scores and academic performance
Graduation and dropout rates
Program participation data




# Load required packages
source("src/R/setup.R")

# Fetch school directory data
schools <- fetch_dataset(NYC_DATA$school_directory)

# Quick overview
glimpse(schools)



edu-analytics/
├── org/                  # Org-mode source files
│   ├── index.org
│   ├── schools.org
│   ├── students.org
│   └── learning.org
├── src/R/               # R scripts and functions
│   ├── setup.R          # Package loading
│   ├── nyc_data.R       # NYC Open Data endpoints
│   └── utils.R          # Helper functions
├── data/                # Local data cache (parquet)
├── output/              # Generated figures
├── docs/                # Quarto output (website)
└── _quarto.yml          # Quarto configuration




Schools Analysis - Building space, locations, facilities
Students Analysis - Enrollment, demographics, attendance
Learning Outcomes - Test scores, graduation rates, academic progress

title: Data Sources
text:
Loading the Data Library
## ============================================================
## Load NYC Open Data library
## ============================================================

source("src/R/setup.R")
source("src/R/nyc_data.R")

# Display available datasets
datasets <- list_datasets()
print(datasets)

title: Data Sources
text:
School Directory
## ============================================================
## School Directory Dataset
## Comprehensive list of all NYC public schools
## ============================================================

info <- get_dataset_info(NYC_DATA$school_directory)
cat("Dataset:", info$name, "\n")
cat("ID:", NYC_DATA$school_directory, "\n")
cat("Last Updated:", format(info$last_updated, "%Y-%m-%d"), "\n")
cat("Columns:", info$columns, "\n")

title: Data Sources
text:
Building Space Usage
## ============================================================
## Building Space Usage Dataset  
## Annual space utilization metrics
## ============================================================

info <- get_dataset_info(NYC_DATA$building_space_usage)
cat("Dataset:", info$name, "\n")
cat("ID:", NYC_DATA$building_space_usage, "\n")
cat("Description:", substr(info$description, 1, 200), "...\n")

title: Data Sources
text:
Demographic Snapshot
## ============================================================
## Demographic Snapshot
## Student demographics by school
## ============================================================

info <- get_dataset_info(NYC_DATA$demographic_snapshot)
cat("Dataset:", info$name, "\n")
cat("ID:", NYC_DATA$demographic_snapshot, "\n")
cat("Use Case: Analyze student population composition\n")

text:

cat("Use Case: Study attendance patterns and chronic absenteeism\n")

cat("Grades: 3-8\n")

title: Data Sources
text:
Basic Fetch
# Fetch with automatic caching
schools <- fetch_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$school_directory,
  cache_name = "school_directory"
)

title: Data Sources
text:
With Filtering (SoQL)
# Server-side filtering
brooklyn_schools <- fetch_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$school_directory,
  `$where` = "boro = 'K'"  # K = Brooklyn
)

title: Data Sources
text:
Large Datasets
# Paginated fetch for large datasets
all_demographics <- fetch_large_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$demographic_snapshot,
  batch_size = 50000,
  cache_name = "demographics_full"
)

title: Schools Analysis
text:
School Directory
## ============================================================
## Fetch NYC School Directory
## Dataset: 2019-2020 School Locations
## ============================================================

schools <- fetch_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$school_directory,
  cache_name = "school_directory"
)

## Display structure
cat("Dataset dimensions:", nrow(schools), "rows x", ncol(schools), "columns\n")
cat("\nColumn names:\n")
print(names(schools))

title: Schools Analysis
text:
Building Space Usage
## ============================================================
## Fetch DOE Building Space Usage
## Updated annually - tracks space utilization in school buildings
## ============================================================

building_space <- fetch_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$building_space_usage,
  cache_name = "building_space"
)

cat("Building space records:", nrow(building_space), "\n")
glimpse(building_space)

title: Schools Analysis
text:
Schools by Borough
## ============================================================
## Count of schools by borough
## ============================================================

schools_by_boro <- schools |>
  count(borough = boro, name = "n_schools") |>
  mutate(borough = fct_reorder(borough, n_schools))

ggplot(schools_by_boro, aes(x = n_schools, y = borough, fill = borough)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_schools), hjust = -0.2, size = 4) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", begin = 0.2, end = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "NYC Public Schools by Borough",
    subtitle = "Source: NYC Open Data School Directory",
    x = "Number of Schools",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = max(schools_by_boro$n_schools) * 1.1)

title: Schools Analysis
text:
School Types Distribution
## ============================================================
## Distribution of school types
## ============================================================

school_types <- schools |>
  count(school_type = schooltype, name = "count") |>
  filter(!is.na(school_type)) |>
  mutate(
    pct = count / sum(count) * 100,
    school_type = fct_reorder(school_type, count)
  )

ggplot(school_types, aes(x = count, y = school_type)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of NYC School Types",
    x = "Number of Schools",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  expand_limits(x = max(school_types$count) * 1.15)

title: Students Analysis
text:
Demographic Snapshot
## ============================================================
## Fetch NYC School Demographics
## Dataset: Demographic snapshot by school
## ============================================================

demographics <- fetch_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$demographic_snapshot,
  cache_name = "demographics"
)

cat("Demographics dataset:", nrow(demographics), "rows\n")
cat("\nAvailable columns:\n")
print(names(demographics))

title: Students Analysis
text:
Enrollment Data

title: Students Analysis
text:
Attendance Data
## ============================================================
## Fetch attendance and chronic absenteeism data
## ============================================================

attendance <- fetch_dataset(
  dataset_id = NYC_DATA$attendance,
  cache_name = "attendance"
)

cat("Attendance records:", nrow(attendance), "\n")

title: Students Analysis
Students Analysis
Students Analysis
text:
Demographic Composition
## ============================================================
## Overall demographic composition
## ============================================================

# Common demographic column patterns
demo_cols <- c("asian", "black", "hispanic", "white", "multi_racial", 
               "native_american", "pacific_islander")

available_demo <- names(demographics)[names(demographics) %in% demo_cols]

if (length(available_demo) > 0) {

  demo_totals <- demographics |>
    summarise(across(all_of(available_demo), 
                     ~sum(as.numeric(.), na.rm = TRUE))) |>
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "group", values_to = "count") |>
    mutate(
      pct = count / sum(count) * 100,
      group = str_to_title(str_replace_all(group, "_", " "))
    )

  ggplot(demo_totals, aes(x = "", y = pct, fill = group)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)),
              position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
              size = 3) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Group") +
    labs(
      title = "NYC Student Demographics",
      subtitle = "Percentage by Ethnicity"
    ) +
    theme_void(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

} else {
  cat("Demographic columns not found. Available columns:\n")
  print(names(demographics))
}

title: Students Analysis
Students Analysis
text:
Chronic Absenteeism
## ============================================================
## Chronic absenteeism analysis
## (Students missing 10%+ of school days)
## ============================================================

if (exists("attendance") && nrow(attendance) > 0) {

  # Look for absenteeism rate columns
  absent_cols <- names(attendance)[grep("absent|chronic|attend", 
                                        names(attendance), 
                                        ignore.case = TRUE)]

  cat("Attendance-related columns:\n")
  print(absent_cols)

  if ("chronic_absenteeism_rate" %in% names(attendance)) {

    absentee_dist <- attendance |>
      filter(!is.na(chronic_absenteeism_rate)) |>
      mutate(rate = as.numeric(chronic_absenteeism_rate))

    ggplot(absentee_dist, aes(x = rate)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "coral", color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
      geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(rate, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                 color = "darkred", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
      labs(
        title = "Distribution of Chronic Absenteeism Rates",
        subtitle = "Dashed line = citywide average",
        x = "Chronic Absenteeism Rate (%)",
        y = "Number of Schools"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  }

} else {
  cat("Attendance data not loaded\n")
}

title: Students Analysis
About
text:
This project explores NYC public education data using literate programming techniques. All analysis is conducted transparently with reproducible code.




All data comes from the NYC Open Data Portal, specifically the Education category maintained by the NYC Department of Education.




Data Acquisition - Fetched via Socrata SODA API with local Parquet caching
Data Cleaning - Standardized borough names, parsed DBN codes, handled missing values
Exploratory Analysis - Summary statistics, distributions, correlations
Visualization - ggplot2 graphics with consistent theming
Reporting - Org-mode → Quarto → HTML publishing













Tool


Version


Purpose






R


4.x


Statistical computing




tidyverse


2.x


Data manipulation




ggplot2


3.x


Visualization




Quarto


1.x


Publishing




Emacs Org-mode


9.x


Literate programming









Data reflects publicly available aggregations; individual student data is protected
Some datasets may have reporting delays or gaps
School-level aggregations may mask within-school variation
Historical data comparability affected by policy and assessment changes




All code is available in the project repository. To reproduce:
git clone <repository>
cd edu-analytics
make setup
make fetch-data
make all



For questions about this analysis, please open an issue on the project repository.




Code: MIT License
Data: NYC Open Data Terms of Use
Content: CC BY 4.0

text:
Math Test Results

title: Learning Outcomes
Learning Outcomes
Learning Outcomes
text:
Test Scores by Borough
## ============================================================
## Test proficiency by borough
## ============================================================

if (exists("math_results") && "borough" %in% names(math_results)) {

  boro_math <- math_results |>
    group_by(borough) |>
    summarise(
      proficiency_rate = sum(as.numeric(level_3_4_count), na.rm = TRUE) /
                        sum(as.numeric(number_tested), na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
      n_schools = n_distinct(dbn),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(borough)) |>
    mutate(borough = fct_reorder(borough, proficiency_rate))

  ggplot(boro_math, aes(x = proficiency_rate, y = borough, fill = borough)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", proficiency_rate)), 
              hjust = -0.1, size = 4) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "mako", begin = 0.2, end = 0.8) +
    labs(
      title = "Math Proficiency by Borough",
      x = "Proficiency Rate (%)",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    expand_limits(x = max(boro_math$proficiency_rate) * 1.15)

}

title: Learning Outcomes
Learning Outcomes
Learning Outcomes
text:
Dropout Analysis
## ============================================================
## Dropout rate analysis
## ============================================================

if (exists("graduation") && "dropout_pct" %in% names(graduation)) {

  dropout_dist <- graduation |>
    filter(!is.na(dropout_pct)) |>
    mutate(dropout_rate = as.numeric(dropout_pct))

  ggplot(dropout_dist, aes(x = dropout_rate)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 25, fill = "firebrick", color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(dropout_rate, na.rm = TRUE)),
               color = "darkred", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(
      title = "Distribution of Dropout Rates",
      subtitle = "NYC High Schools (dashed line = mean)",
      x = "Dropout Rate (%)",
      y = "Number of Schools"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

}

title: Learning Outcomes
Learning Outcomes
text:
Performance Gap by Economic Status
## ============================================================
## Achievement gap analysis
## Compare economically disadvantaged vs all students
## ============================================================

if (exists("math_results")) {

  # Check for economic need indicator
  econ_cols <- names(math_results)[grep("econ|poverty|free_lunch|need", 
                                         names(math_results), 
                                         ignore.case = TRUE)]

  if (length(econ_cols) > 0) {
    cat("Economic indicator columns found:\n")
    print(econ_cols)

    # Analysis would depend on actual column structure
    # Placeholder for gap visualization
  } else {
    cat("Economic indicator not found in test data\n")
    cat("Available columns:\n")
    print(names(math_results))
  }

}
